en: You guys handle 80% of the intelligence workload.
You're seven times the size of the CIA. We don't like to brag about that, Will.
So the way I see it, the question isn't, why should you work for the NSA?
The question is, why shouldn't you?
ko: 질문은, 왜 일하지 말아야 하냐는 거지.

en: Why shouldn't I work for the NSA? It's a tough one.
ko: 왜 내가 NSA에서 일하지 말아야 하지?

en: But I'll take a shot. Say I'm working at the NSA and somebody puts a code on my desk, something no one else can break.
ko: 내가 NSA에서 일하고 누군가 내 책상에 코드를 놓는다고 해,

en: Maybe I take a shot at it, maybe I break it. And I'm real happy with myself because I did my job well.
ko: 아마 내가 시도해보고, 아마 풀 수도 있어.

en: But maybe that code was the location of some rebel army in North Africa or the Middle East.
ko: 하지만 그 코드가 북아프리카나 중동의 반군 위치일 수도 있어.

en: And once they have that location, they bomb the village where the rebels are hiding.
ko: 그 위치를 알게 되면, 반군이 숨고 있는 마을을 폭격해.

en: Fifteen hundred people that I never met, never had no problem with get killed.
ko: 내가 한 번도 만나본 적 없는 1,500명이 죽어.

en: Now the politicians are saying, oh, send in the Marines to secure the area because they don't give a shit.
It won't be their kid over there getting shot, just like it wasn't them when their number got called
ko: 거기서 총에 맞는 건 그들의 자식이 아니니까,

en: because they were all pulling a tour in the National Guard.
ko: 그들은 모두 주 방위군에서 복무 중이었으니까.

en: It'll be some kid from Southie over there taking shrapnel in the ass.
ko: 거기서 파편에 맞는 건 사우스이에서 온 어떤 아이일 거야.
